author: davidclin
text:
I'm doing a fresh install of the c7n\_mailer tool from master and encountering `ImportError: No module named c7n.mu` when executing the command c7n-mailer:

```
(c7n_mailer) [ec2-user@cloudcustodian-mfa-launcher cloud-custodian]$ c7n-mailer
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/usr/bin/c7n-mailer", line 11, in 
    load_entry_point('c7n-mailer', 'console_scripts', 'c7n-mailer')()
  File "/usr/lib/python2.7/site-packages/pkg_resources/__init__.py", line 572, in load_entry_point
    return get_distribution(dist).load_entry_point(group, name)
  File "/usr/lib/python2.7/site-packages/pkg_resources/__init__.py", line 2755, in load_entry_point
    return ep.load()
  File "/usr/lib/python2.7/site-packages/pkg_resources/__init__.py", line 2408, in load
    return self.resolve()
  File "/usr/lib/python2.7/site-packages/pkg_resources/__init__.py", line 2414, in resolve
    module = __import__(self.module_name, fromlist=['__name__'], level=0)
  File "/home/ec2-user/cloud-custodian/tools/c7n_mailer/c7n_mailer/cli.py", line 10, in 
    from c7n_mailer import deploy, utils
  File "/home/ec2-user/cloud-custodian/tools/c7n_mailer/c7n_mailer/deploy.py", line 20, in 
    from c7n.mu import (
ImportError: No module named c7n.mu
```

There are the steps I used:

```
*** Install repository***
$ git clone https://github.com/capitalone/cloud-custodian

*** Install dependencies (with virtualenv) ***
$ virtualenv c7n_mailer
$ source c7n_mailer/bin/activate
$ cd cloud-custodian/tools/c7n_mailer
$ pip install -r requirements.txt

*** Install extensions ***
$ sudo python setup.py develop

*** Verify Installation ***
$ c7n-mailer
$ custodian
```

What am I doing wrong?

I'm using the latest Amazon Linux2 AMI, ami-009d6802948d06e52 (64-bit x86).

author: kapilt
text: i think its the sudo command thats causing problems, as it will exit the virtualenv

author: kapilt
text: fwiw, i dropped the sudo and ran the same in amazon linux docker container without issue, i'd suggest doing the same, extensions compilation and venv install doesn't need sudo.

author: kapilt
text: if you really do need sudo you should give full path to venv binary on python, and be prepared to chmod depending on your default umask for the venv installs.

author: davidclin
text: Thanks. The `sudo` was what did me in. Appreciate the help!
